title: gulp.watch() error message is confusing when passed a named task string
body: Calling `gulp.watch('src/**/*.js', 'build')` throws "watching src/**/*.js: watch task has to be a function..." from index.js, but it doesn't mention that you can pass `gulp.series('build')` instead, which is what most people actually need. Could the error suggest the fix directly?

title: index.mjs ESM entry point missing lastRun/tree exports
body: Importing gulp via `import gulp from 'gulp'` in an ESM gulpfile works for src/dest/task/series/parallel, but `gulp.lastRun` and `gulp.tree` referenced from index.mjs don't behave the same as the CommonJS `require('gulp')` instance — looks like the mjs wrapper may not be re-exporting the full instance surface.

title: No guidance on running a local gulpfile without a global gulp install
body: bin/gulp.js is just `require('gulp-cli')()`, which is fine, but there's nothing in this repo pointing new users to `npx gulp` for running a local devDependency gulpfile without `npm install -g gulp`. Could a short comment or doc pointer be added?

title: test/watch.js intermittently fails on CI
body: test/watch.js seems to rely on real filesystem event timing to assert that watch() triggered the task, and on our slower CI runners it flakes maybe 1 in 20 runs with no changed files. Could these tests use fake timers or an explicit ready callback instead of relying on wall-clock delays?

title: watch() always wraps the task in parallel(), losing series() ordering guarantees
body: Gulp.prototype.watch in index.js does `fn = this.parallel(task)` whenever a function is passed directly. If someone passes a function that itself calls other tasks expecting sequential semantics, wrapping it in parallel() unexpectedly changes execution order versus calling gulp.series() manually outside watch().
